content: Evidence-based grounding: 5-4-3-2-1 (name 5 things you see, 4 hear, 3 touch, 2 smell, 1 taste). Box breathing: inhale 4s, hold 4s, exhale 4s, hold 4s. NHS recommends talking therapies (CBT) for persistent anxiety. Self-refer via NHS talking therapies or GP.

Low mood lasting 2+ weeks, loss of interest, sleep/appetite changes, or thoughts of self-harm warrant professional support. UK: GP can refer; NHS Talking Therapies (self-referral); Samaritans 116 123 for emotional support. Exercise, routine, and daylight exposure can help alongside treatment.

Sleep hygiene: fixed wake time, dark cool room, no screens 1h before bed, limit caffeine after midday, avoid alcohol as sleep aid. If insomnia persists weeks, see GP—CBT-I is first-line for chronic insomnia in UK.
Grief has no fixed timeline; anger, guilt, numbness are common. Cruse Bereavement Care UK: 0808 808 1677. NHS bereavement pages list local services. Peer support and counselling can help when stuck.

If at risk of harm: Samaritans 116 123, Shout 85258, A&E or 999 in emergency. Alternatives to self-harm include ice on skin, intense exercise, cold water on face (TIPP skills). GP or crisis team for ongoing risk.
Beat UK eating disorder charity: 0808 801 0677 (England), similar lines for NI/Scotland/Wales on beateatingdisorders.org.uk. Early treatment improves outcomes; GP can refer to specialist services.

After betrayal or breakup: no contact can aid healing; lean on trusted friends; Relate.org.uk offers counselling. It's normal to cycle through denial, anger, sadness. Professional support if stuck or unsafe.

Loneliness is a health risk but treatable. Small steps: volunteer, local groups, online communities with moderation. Mind UK resources on loneliness. Age UK and local councils run befriending schemes.

Work-related stress: ACAS (acas.org.uk) for UK employment rights; occupational health via employer; reasonable adjustments for mental health under Equality Act. Boundaries, breaks, and saying no where safe to do so.

PTSD and trauma responses are treatable with trauma-focused CBT or EMDR via NHS or private. Grounding during flashbacks; avoid self-blame. Rape Crisis England & Wales 0808 802 9999 for sexual violence support.
Young people: Childline 0800 1111, Papyrus HopelineUK 0800 068 4141 (under 35 suicide prevention). CAMHS via GP for under-18s mental health in NHS.
This chatbot cannot prescribe or adjust medication. Medication decisions belong to a qualified prescriber (GP/psychiatrist). Never stop psychiatric medication abruptly without medical advice.
UK NHS Talking Therapies (IAPT in England) offers CBT, counselling, guided self-help—often self-referral. Private: BACP and UKCP registers list accredited therapists. Wait times vary by area.
